input_dict.agent_role: Produce one compact second-hop retrieval query targeting the missing bridge or supporting evidence.

task_context.question: Were Ivan Perestiani and Patricio Guzmán both directors?
task_context.agent_role: Produce one compact second-hop retrieval query targeting the missing bridge or supporting evidence.

visible_agent_inputs.question: Were Ivan Perestiani and Patricio Guzmán both directors?
visible_agent_inputs.summary_1:
First-hop evidence: Ivan Perestiani — Passage "Ivan Perestiani": "was a Russian/Soviet film director" (lead/occupation). Patricio Guzmán — Passage "Patricio Guzmán": "is a Chilean documentary film director" (lead/occupation).

Bridge needed: Verify that both Ivan Perestiani and Patricio Guzmán are listed with "film director" (or "documentary film director") in their biographical/infobox occupation fields.

Retrieval direction: 1. Fetch the page titled "Ivan Perestiani" (e.g., Wikipedia) and inspect the lead sentence and infobox 'occupation' or filmography for "film director". 2. Fetch the page titled "Patricio Guzmán" and inspect the lead sentence and infobox 'occupation' or filmography for "documentary film director".

current_output.query:
1) Retrieve the Wikipedia page for "Ivan Perestiani" (en.wikipedia.org). Return the page title, the full lead sentence/first paragraph, and the infobox "occupation" field (or occupation lines). If the infobox is not present, return any explicit occupation wording from the lead paragraph.

2) Retrieve the Wikipedia page for "Patricio Guzmán" (en.wikipedia.org). Return the page title, the full lead sentence/first paragraph, and the infobox "occupation" field (or occupation lines). If the infobox is not present, return any explicit occupation wording from the lead paragraph.

2) Retrieve the Wikipedia page for "Patricio Guzmán" (en.wikipedia.org). Return the page title, the full lead sentence/first paragraph, and the infobox "occupation" field (or occupation lines). If the infobox is not present, return any explicit occupation wording from the lead paragraph.

retrieved_doc_snippets: Infobox | An infobox is a template used to collect and present a subset of information about its subject, such as a document. It is a structured document containing a set of attribute–value pairs, and in Wikipedia represents a summary of information about the subject of an article. In this way, they are comparable to data tables in some aspects. When presented within the larger document it summarizes, an infobox is often presented in a sidebar format.

List of people from Kent | This is a list of notable residents of the county of Kent in England who have a Wikipedia page. Persons are grouped by occupation and listed in order of birth. Kent is defined by its current boundaries.

Document retrieval | Document retrieval is defined as the matching of some stated user query against a set of free-text records. These records could be any type of mainly unstructured text, such as newspaper articles, real estate records or paragraphs in a manual. User queries can range from multi-sentence full descriptions of an information need to a few words.

Opening sentence | At the beginning of a written work stands the opening sentence. The opening line is part or all of the opening sentence that may start the lead paragraph. For older texts the Latin term "incipit" (it begins) is in use for the very first words of the opening sentence.

List of Malayalam-language authors | List of Malayalam-language authors lists writers in Malayalam who already have Wikipedia pages. References for the information appear on the linked Wikipedia pages. The list is incomplete – please help to expand it by adding Wikipedia page-owning writers who have written extensively in any genre or field, including science and scholarship. Please follow the entry format.

List of English writers | List of English writers lists writers in English, born or raised in England (or who lived in England for a lengthy period), who already have Wikipedia pages. References for the information here appear on the linked Wikipedia pages. The list is incomplete – please help to expand it by adding Wikipedia page-owning writers who have written extensively in any genre or field, including science and scholarship. Please follow the entry format. A seminal work added to a writer' ...[truncated]

Wikiseek | Wikiseek was a search engine that indexed English Wikipedia pages and pages that were linked to from Wikipedia articles. The search engine was funded by a Palo Alto based Internet startup SearchMe and was officially launched on January 17, 2007. Most of the funding came from Sequoia Capital. It used Google ads on its search returns to generate profit. As of 2008 it is no longer active.

output.query:
site:wikipedia.org Ivan Perestiani director occupation
site:wikipedia.org Patricio Guzmán documentary director occupation

retrieved_doc_snippets: Wikidumper.org | WikiDumper.org is a website created by Clifford A. Pickover that promises to permanently record a snapshot of the "best of the Wikipedia rejects", articles that are slated for deletion at the site. WikiDumper was launched on November 4, 2006, and accepts user submissions. Although the site doesn't specify its criteria for inclusion, many of its articles don't cite their sources. The site has been criticized as likely to be less accurate than Wikipedia.

Robert Jessup | Robert Jessup (born July 18, 1952) is an American painter. Although currently working in the abstract style he painted for most of his career in the figurative style (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Figurative_art), particularly in large triptych panels (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Triptych).
Patricio Guzmán | Patricio Guzmán Lozanes (born August 11, 1941) is a Chilean documentary film director. He is internationally renowned for films such as "The Battle of Chile" and "Salvador Allende".

URL redirection | URL redirection, also called URL forwarding, is a World Wide Web technique for making a web page available under more than one URL address. When a web browser attempts to open a URL that has been redirected, a page with a different URL is opened. Similarly, domain redirection or domain forwarding is when all pages in a URL domain are redirected to a different domain, as when wikipedia.com and wikipedia.net are automatically redirected to wikipedia.org. URL redirection is done f ...[truncated]

Wikipediocracy | Wikipediocracy is a website for discussion and criticism of Wikipedia. Its members have brought information about Wikipedia's controversies to the attention of the media. The site was founded in March 2012 by users of Wikipedia Review, another site critical of Wikipedia.

Zeno.org | Zeno.org (] ) is a digital library with German texts and other content such as pictures, facsimile, etc., which has been started by the Zenodot Verlagsgesellschaft mbH, a German publishing house and sister enterprise of Directmedia Publishing GmbH. The content is based on the digital libraries from the CDs and DVDs published by Directmedia, The Yorck Projekt and Zenodot. It uses a stable mirror of the German Wikipedia, purchasable as German language Wikipedia DVD-ROM. Since its offici ...[truncated]
